title: glycosmis parviflora var. obtusa
intro: Glycosmis parviflora var. obtusa is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, green leaves and fragrant flowers. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Glycosmis parviflora var. obtusa include the relief of coughs, colds, and flu symptoms, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
